_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-25 MI-POST (Revised 2/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: MI-POST — Michigan Physician Orders for Scope of Treatment
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-25: MI-POST — MDHHS Approved 2/24/2023; MCL 333.20967; Public Act 154 of 2017
purpose: The MI-POST (Michigan Physician Orders for Scope of Treatment) is a physician order form that documents a patient's preferences for resuscitation and treatment scope. MI-POST has LEGAL FORCE as a physician order. EMS must follow a valid MI-POST form. The original form is printed on pink paper, but paper or digital copies are also valid.

sections.title: MI-POST Form Validity
points: Form must be dated within the last year OR have a reaffirmation date within the last year
Must be signed by a licensed health professional (physician, PA, or NP)
Must be signed by the patient, patient advocate, guardian, or parent of a minor
Original form is printed on PINK paper — however, paper copies and digital/electronic copies are VALID
A verbal order from the ordering health professional is valid for 72 hours pending receipt of written form
Patient may revoke MI-POST at any time — see revocation section

sections.title: Section A — Pulseless or Not Breathing
points: ATTEMPT RESUSCITATION: treat per Cardiac Arrest Protocol — initiate CPR and full resuscitative measures
DO NOT ATTEMPT resuscitation: treat per Dead on Scene / Termination of Resuscitation Protocol — do not initiate CPR

sections.title: Section B — Patient Has Pulse and is Breathing
points: COMFORT-FOCUSED treatment (Comfort Measures Only):
— Palliative and comfort care only
— If breathing stops and patient is unconscious: do NOT assist ventilation
— Oxygen for comfort and pain management per protocol
SELECTIVE treatment:
— Treat per appropriate protocol
— Establish IV access
— Cardiac monitoring
— Non-invasive airway support (BVM, CPAP)
— NO invasive airways — supraglottic airways and endotracheal intubation are PROHIBITED
FULL TREATMENT:
— All interventions including intubation and advanced airway management
— Treat per appropriate protocol without limitation
BLANK Section B: treat as FULL TREATMENT — implied full treatment when Section B is not completed

sections.title: Revocation
points: The patient, patient advocate, guardian, or parent of a minor may revoke MI-POST at any time
Revocation may be verbal, written, or by any action demonstrating intent to revoke
Upon revocation: immediately begin resuscitation or full treatment per appropriate protocol
Contact Medical Control after initiating care
Document the revocation, who revoked, and circumstances in PCR

deterioration_flags: MI-POST not found or not valid — treat patient per appropriate protocol; contact Medical Control if uncertain
MI-POST revoked — initiate full resuscitation/treatment per appropriate protocol; contact MC
MI-POST blank in Section B — treat as FULL TREATMENT implied
Comfort-Focused with breathing stop and unconsciousness — do NOT assist ventilation per MI-POST; provide oxygen and comfort care
scope_notes: MI-POST is a legal physician order — must be followed when valid
Valid: dated within last year (or reaffirmation within last year); signed by health professional AND patient/advocate/guardian/parent
Pink original AND paper/digital copies are valid
Verbal order valid 72 hours pending written form
Section A Attempt Resuscitation → Cardiac Arrest Protocol
Section A DNR → Dead on Scene/TOR Protocol
Section B Comfort-Focused → palliative only; no assisted ventilation if breathing stops and unconscious
Section B Selective → protocol treatment + IV + cardiac monitor + non-invasive airway; NO invasive airways including SGA
Section B Full or Blank → full treatment including intubation
Revocation: initiate full care; contact MC
